Important: Please Save Your Own Copy Before Editing :) 



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #2 

The Simon Cowell Qualifier



Ry Schwartz & Copyhackers

2020



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #2

About The “Simon Cowell Qualifier”

The Persuasion Deep Dive

Where to send it:

Themeplate

See it in action:

Example: Copy Hackers - 10x Freelance Copywriter (October 2018)



About The “Simon Cowell Qualifier” 

The Simon Cowell Qualifier is used to call your audience to a higher game. 



It’s about transcending mediocre or lower level goals/outcomes that they’re used to setting for themselves (or seeing from your competitors) -- and instead, inviting them to use your product/service ONLY if they’re ready to meet your standard of success.



In that sense, you’re qualifying them --  and being almost unreasonably harsh in who qualifies and who doesn’t (thus the Simon Cowell namesake). 







In doing so, you position yourself/your brand against lower level standards and/or “beginner” goals that no longer apply to them while reminding them that they’ve transcended those outcomes and have EARNED the right to be more ambitious (in “coaching the conversion terminology, you’re exclusively empowering them to take action on the next set of goals). 



At its core, The SCQ is about holding your audience to a higher standard -- and in doing so, having them scale up their standards while realizing they’ve maybe settled for less valuable outcomes or inferior results with competitors. 



When you’re the first to coach this “shift” and insight -- you in turn create a new DESIRE  that you’re exclusively and immediately positioned to fulfill. 



The Persuasion Deep Dive

One of the greatest persuasion lessons I learnt while studying the industry’s top enrollment specialists was this: 



“Your first job is to enroll your prospect in a better, more successful version of themselves.”  (credit: Alex Moscow of The Congruent Coach).



That’s what we’re “coaching” in this themeplate. And we’re doing it by:



Exclusive Empowerment: Based on results they’ve already achieved and/or higher standards they have for themselves. 



In qualifying them, they distance from the lower outcomes: By speaking unfavorably of those lower outcomes -- they’ll want to “distance” themselves as fast  as humanly possible by ACCEPTING the new list of standards you offer them.    



Awakening Higher Desire: This themeplate is designed to be the pivotal moment in their journey where they call themselves to a higher game. Where they realize how they’ve been settling, and how perhaps they’ve earned the right to aim higher. This is a powerful shift that immediately CREATES a desire gap (aka… i desire this new outcome -- but have no idea  how to attain it)... which makes your offer immediately relevant and attractive.



A “mid-cro”commitment: In the Simon Cowell Qualifier, the call to action isn’t as  important as the “call to commitment”. When we can get our prospect to commit to bigger outcomes (outcome that we, and we alone, can fulfill), this acts as a powerful precursor to the main conversion. 



Establishing consistency with new outcomes: Now that they’ve committed to this “better” version of themselves (whatever that looks like for you)... your product or service will act as the bridge between those two realities. 

 

Where to send it:

As an invitation to a conversion event (ie. webinar invite)

As a standalone sales email



Message Mining Q’s:

What has my ideal client already (and recently) achieved?

How do they experience those achievements? (mentally/physically/emotionally)

What’s the next level of attainment (or outcome) that they may not have accepted for themselves yet?

How would they experience those new outcomes? 

What are lower level standards the market has for your type of product/service?

What’s a higher marker of success you can call them to? 

Themeplate

[List Lower-Level Outcomes / things they’ve already achieved]

[Flip the script]

[Call them to a higher game]

[List new standards --  higher outcomes]

[Contrast the two]

[CTA framed as a bridge between the lower and higher outcomes]

[Qualify them based on goals/ambition/desires outcome]

[Final CTA]

See it in action: 

Example: Copy Hackers - 10x Freelance Copywriter (October 2018) 



Subject line:

“Remember When”



Body:

Remember when…

… you got to flip off Jerry The Middle Manager as you quit your job and headed into your freelance copywriting career? 

… sipping pumpkin spice lattes on a Tuesday AM was all the fulfillment you’d ever need from the #freelance life?

… snagging a 3-day weekend ((sans HR sign-off)) was the ULTIMATE expression of William Wallace approved freeeeeeedom? 

No? 

Maybe that's because you didn’t get into the freelance game for the cheap satisfaction of spending your days working in a damn Snuggie.

Or for the sicker satisfaction of slapping keys at your local Starbucks, knowing your ex-colleagues were rotting away in a cubicled Shawshank. 

Like a deep-fried Mars bar, those “small wins” satisfied you for all of 13 seconds. 

They're not why you're a freelance copywriter today.

You have much loftier expectations of yourself. 

'Round these parts, we'd call 'em 10x-pectations. 

You got into this freelance copy game because you wanted to make REAL dollahs while making a REAL impact… and enjoying the REAL perks that come with it.

You want to build a business - not just a side gig - with staying power. 

One that grows in revenue month after month.

Commanding bigger fees from better clients as your confidence trends upwards. 

Putting a big ol' end to the "starving writer" myth.

And hell… 

If it weren’t for these 4 dead obvious freelancing mistakes that I’ll save you from this Tuesday…

You might actually be well on your way to achieving everything you know, deep inside, you could be getting from your freelance life... instead of using your creative juices to pen an apology letter to ol' Jerry because your once-supportive spouse is now passively aggressively "suggesting" it's time to beg Jerry to let you go back to that cubicled Shawshank.

#F*ckJerry

Here’s the deal.

This Tuesday, at 9am PST / 12pm EST, I’m hosting a live freelance copywriter workshop over here. 

This one’s for my 10x-pectations freelance copywriters. 

Not the run-of-the-mill “look at me ma, I landed a sweet $10 gig on Upwork” freelance copywriter. 

But my “I’ve chalked up some wins. I got more to give. So how do I literally 10x my business in 2018?”freelance copywriter.

If that’s you, let’s talk for an hour. 

Pumpkin spice latte optional.

Big time ambition, not. 

~jo

PS: Nothing against the $10-gig freelancer, BTW! I just have high expectations of life + an insatiable hunger... which means I can better teach copywriters who have high expectations + an insatiable hunger. If that's you but you're still accepting cheap Upwork gigs, you should def come to this Tuesday's session. 'Cos yeah... it's time to stop taking cheap Upwork gigs.

Secure a spot here
